findings.title: Migration missing rollback file — breaks project pattern
findings.why_it_matters: Every other migration in the project has a corresponding rollback file in supabase/migrations/rollback/. The retention_audit_log migration has none. In the event of a failed deployment or needing to revert, the operator has no rollback script. The migration creates a non-trivial table with indexes and RLS policies that would need to be carefully dropped in the right order.
findings.suggested_fix: Create supabase/migrations/rollback/20260501000001_retention_audit_log_rollback.sql with: BEGIN; DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_retention_audit_log_run_id; DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_retention_audit_log_completed_at; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS public.retention_audit_log; COMMIT;
evidence: ls supabase/migrations/rollback/ shows every migration from 20260413 through 20260429 has a _rollback.sql file

residual_risks: The retention_audit_log table accumulates rows indefinitely. A 1-year rolling purge is deferred to a future story. For now, monitoring table size is advisable.
